hyperedges.label: Prisma 7 architecture: config, generator, adapter, raw SQL discipline
Dockerized dev/prod stack (app + postgres + minio + createbuckets)

nodes.label: KSPlatform Stack (Next 16, React 19, Prisma 7, Postgres 16, Auth.js v5, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, Tiptap 3, MinIO, zod)
nodes.norm_label: ksplatform stack (next 16, react 19, prisma 7, postgres 16, auth.js v5, tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, tiptap 3, minio, zod)

nodes.label: Next 16 middleware→proxy gotcha
nodes.rationale: Next 16 removes middleware.ts; auth must be enforced in pages/actions via requireUserId() instead of a proxy, to keep auth checks explicit and colocated with mutations.

nodes.label: Async params/searchParams gotcha
nodes.rationale: Next 16 makes params/searchParams Promises; pages must await them.

nodes.label: Prisma 7 conventions
nodes.rationale: Prisma 7 moves the datasource URL out of schema.prisma into prisma.config.ts, and generates the client into src/generated/prisma via the prisma-client generator, imported as @/generated/prisma/client rather than @prisma/client.

nodes.label: pg_trgm GIN indexes (tag_name_trgm_idx, post_title_trgm_idx, tweet_body_trgm_idx)
nodes.rationale: These GIN trigram indexes aren't expressible in schema.prisma, so migrate dev emits spurious DROP INDEX statements; those must be deleted before applying a migration, or the search-powering indexes get destroyed.

nodes.label: $queryRaw bound-params rule
nodes.rationale: Raw SQL must use Prisma's tagged-template $queryRaw with bound ${value} params (never $queryRawUnsafe or string concatenation) to prevent SQL injection; user query length is also capped.

nodes.label: Tailwind v4 CSS-first config
nodes.rationale: Tailwind v4 config lives in globals.css (@theme, @plugin, @custom-variant) instead of tailwind.config.js.

nodes.label: React Compiler lint (react-hooks/*)
nodes.rationale: Forbids synchronous setState in an effect body, mutating external values during render, and calling impure functions like Date.now() in the component body; such reads must go in a plain async helper instead.

nodes.label: "use server" files export-only-async rule
nodes.rationale: Server action files may only export async functions, so shared non-async helpers/types/consts must live in src/lib/* instead of src/actions/*.

nodes.label: Server Actions convention (src/actions/*)
nodes.rationale: Mutations go through Server Actions, each re-checking auth/ownership via requireUserId(), validating with zod, then revalidatePath() or returning {error}; REST endpoints are avoided for mutations to keep this pattern uniform.

nodes.label: Route Handlers convention (src/app/api/*)
nodes.rationale: Route handlers are reserved for Auth.js callbacks, image upload (multipart), and tag search (GET) — cases that don't fit the Server Action mutation pattern.

nodes.label: Posts stored as Markdown (Post.contentMd)
nodes.rationale: Storing posts as Markdown lets the editor convert HTML↔MD via turndown/marked and enables one-click Markdown export, while published posts render via react-markdown.

nodes.label: Unified home timeline (loadTimeline)
nodes.rationale: Home feed merges posts and tweets and paginates by an ISO-timestamp cursor so mixed-type ordering stays correct; per-entity feeds use simpler id cursors since they don't need to interleave types.

nodes.label: Moderation convention (owner or admin delete)
nodes.rationale: Delete actions allow owner or admin, with canModerate passed down to cards/sections so admins get inline delete controls without a separate admin-only code path.

nodes.label: shadcn/ui primitives convention
nodes.rationale: Prefer existing shadcn primitives in src/components/ui/, adding new ones via the shadcn CLI, to keep UI consistent; icons from lucide-react, toasts from sonner.

nodes.label: Appearance: 9 composable axes
nodes.rationale: Each axis (base, accent, size, font, surface, radius, card, border, shadow) owns disjoint CSS tokens so preferences compose freely instead of overriding each other; seeded server-side as data-* attrs to avoid a flash of unstyled/wrong theme.

nodes.label: MinIO S3_ENDPOINT vs S3_PUBLIC_URL distinction
nodes.rationale: S3_ENDPOINT is the server-to-server address (minio:9000 in Docker) while S3_PUBLIC_URL is what the browser fetches images from; conflating them breaks image loading in the browser.

nodes.label: Request & rendering model (App Router, server-first)
nodes.rationale: Pages are dynamic Server Components reading session/DB directly; mutations go through Server Actions instead of REST so auth/ownership re-checks live next to the mutation logic; route handlers are reserved for non-action HTTP needs.

nodes.label: Authentication & roles (Auth.js v5, Credentials, JWT)
nodes.rationale: JWT session strategy avoids DB session tables; role lives in the JWT so a hand-edited DB role only takes effect on next login, and the app stays stateless/scalable.

nodes.label: Image uploads (MinIO/S3)
nodes.rationale: Server talks to MinIO over the internal Docker network (S3_ENDPOINT) but must return browser-reachable URLs (S3_PUBLIC_URL); these differ in Docker on purpose.

nodes.label: Editor & Markdown pipeline
nodes.rationale: Storing posts as Markdown makes Export-to-.md trivial; the Tiptap editor converts HTML↔MD via turndown/marked so the DB stays the single source of truth in a portable format.

nodes.label: Unified timeline architecture
nodes.rationale: loadTimeline queries posts and tweets separately then merges by activity time, paginating with an ISO-timestamp cursor so the merged feed still returns strictly-older pages.

nodes.label: Infinite scroll pattern (InfiniteList + IntersectionObserver)
nodes.rationale: A generic client component renders an SSR first page then uses an IntersectionObserver sentinel to call a loadMore(cursor) server action, so every list (posts, tweets, people) shares one pagination implementation.

nodes.label: Admin & moderation (/admin dashboard)
nodes.rationale: requireAdmin() guards the dashboard; delete actions allow owner-or-admin so moderation controls appear inline on content rather than only in a separate admin UI.

nodes.label: Search & tag trends (pg_trgm)
nodes.rationale: pg_trgm powers typo-tolerant fuzzy search across tag/post/tweet lookups via similarity(); all raw SQL uses bound tagged-template queries and length-capped input to avoid injection.

nodes.label: Theming & fonts (9 composable axes)
nodes.rationale: Each axis is a disjoint set of CSS tokens driven by a data-* attribute so axes stack cleanly; root layout seeds them server-side per account to avoid theme flash, persisted via updatePreferencesAction.

nodes.label: Notifications
nodes.rationale: Publishing a post/tweet fans out one Notification per tag subscriber (deduped, excluding the author) via a shared helper, keeping fan-out logic in one place for both content types.

nodes.label: Production deployment notes
nodes.rationale: App is stateless (JWT sessions) so it scales horizontally behind a load balancer; Postgres/MinIO are the shared state; npm install (not npm ci) is used in Docker deps stage because the macOS-generated lockfile omits Linux-only optional deps.

hyperedges.label: Prisma 7 architecture: config, generator, adapter, raw SQL discipline
Dockerized dev/prod stack (app + postgres + minio + createbuckets)
